heading: False advertisements; injunctions and restraining orders
label: 53 False advertisements; injunctions and restraining orders
notes: Amendments1994—Subsecs. (a), (b). , in concluding provisions, substituted “Any suit may be brought where such person, partnership, or corporation resides or transacts business, or wherever venue is proper under . In addition, the court may, if the court determines that the interests of justice require that any other person, partnership, or corporation should be a party in such suit, cause such other person, partnership, or corporation to be added as a party without regard to whether venue is otherwise proper in the district in which the suit is brought. In any suit under this section, process may be served on any person, partnership, or corporation wherever it may be found.” for “Any such suit shall be brought in the district in which such person, partnership, or corporation resides or transacts business.”Pub. (b) and redesignated former subsec. (b) as (c).Pub. L. 93–153

children.heading: Power of Commission; jurisdiction of courts
children.label: a Power of Commission; jurisdiction of courts

children.text: that any person, partnership, or corporation is engaged in, or is about to engage in, the dissemination or the causing of the dissemination of any advertisement in violation of , andsection 52 of this title

that the enjoining thereof pending the issuance of a complaint by the Commission under , and until such complaint is dismissed by the Commission or set aside by the court on review, or the order of the Commission to cease and desist made thereon has become final within the meaning of , would be to the interest of the public,section 45 of this titlesection 45 of this title

children.heading: Temporary restraining orders; preliminary injunctions
children.label: b Temporary restraining orders; preliminary injunctions

children.text: that any person, partnership, or corporation is violating, or is about to violate, any provision of law enforced by the Federal Trade Commission, and

that the enjoining thereof pending the issuance of a complaint by the Commission and until such complaint is dismissed by the Commission or set aside by the court on review, or until the order of the Commission made thereon has become final, would be in the interest of the public—

children.heading: Service of process; proof of service
children.label: c Service of process; proof of service

children.text: by delivering a copy of such process to the person to be served, to a member of the partnership to be served, or to the president, secretary, or other executive officer or a director of the corporation to be served;
by leaving a copy of such process at the residence or the principal office or place of business of such person, partnership, or corporation; or

by mailing a copy of such process by registered mail or certified mail addressed to such person, partnership, or corporation at his, or her, or its residence, principal office, or principal place or business.
that restraining the dissemination of a false advertisement in any particular issue of such publication would delay the delivery of such issue after the regular time therefor, and

that such delay would be due to the method by which the manufacture and distribution of such publication is customarily conducted by the publisher in accordance with sound business practice, and not to any method or device adopted for the evasion of this section or to prevent or delay the issuance of an injunction or restraining order with respect to such false advertisement or any other advertisement,
